title: Fundamental Duties
lead: ==Part IVA, Art 51A== — eleven duties, added on the ==Swaran Singh Committee's== recommendation. Non-justiciable, yet increasingly cited by courts. The SC in [[Durga Dutt v. UoI]] is hearing whether they can be made ==legally enforceable==.

head: Case
Relevance to Fundamental Duties
rows: Connected ==national awards== to Art 51A(j) — striving for excellence.
Environmental harm by hotel developers — relied on ==Art 51A(g)== read with Art 48A and Art 21.
FRs must be read ==with DPSPs and Fundamental Duties== — rights cannot be viewed in isolation from duties.
Directed the Centre to implement the ==Justice J.S. Verma Committee== recommendations on duty awareness.

items.title: Non-Justiciable
bullets: Not legally enforceable — ==moral obligations== with no legal compulsion.

items.title: Vague
bullets: Broad phrasing (‘strive towards excellence’) is ==hard to interpret and implement==.

items.title: Limited Scope
bullets: Omit civic essentials like ==paying taxes and voting== — recommended by Swaran Singh but not included.

items.title: Overlap & Redundancy
bullets: Several duties duplicate existing laws and policies.

items.title: Placement
bullets: Sit after the DPSPs (Part IVA) — ==perceived as less significant== than Fundamental Rights.

items: ==Courts' stance==: duties ==guide==, they don't create directly enforceable obligations ([[AIIMS Students' Union]]). Making them punishable risks ==over-criminalisation== and conflicts with FRs.

==Better route — translate select duties into law==: Art 51A(g) → Environment (Protection) Act regime, noise rules and fireworks time bands ([[In Re: Noise Pollution (2005)]]), environmental-education orders ([[M.C. Mehta (1991)]]).
Use duties to justify ==proportionate restrictions== on FRs: [[Naveen Jindal (2004)]] balanced flag dignity with free expression.
==Justice J.S. Verma Committee (1999)==: curricula, public campaigns — about ==civic culture, not criminal liability== (also mooted duties to vote, participate, pay taxes).

blocks.text: Rights give us freedom, but duties give that freedom meaning.
